title: Calibrating the Confidence of Large Language Models by Eliciting Fidelity

method.problem: RLHF-optimized language models exhibit overconfidence issues where the probability of generating a token doesn't match the likelihood of it being correct
method.baseline: Pre-trained language models have well-calibrated confidence scores through per-token logits

proposed_solution.approach: Decompose model confidence into two dimensions: uncertainty about the question and fidelity to the answer
proposed_solution.uncertainty_measurement: Information entropy of frequency distribution of sampled answers
proposed_solution.fidelity_assessment: Replace model's answer with 'All other options are wrong' to assess fidelity

visualizations: Figure 1: Reliability diagram showing calibration effects

related_work: Aggregation of logit-based and verbalization-based confidence
